title: France's military spending plans face reality of fragile national finances
subhead: The government's defense objectives are incompatible with meeting European public spending commitments, France's main audit institution has warned, pointing to 'certainly difficult choices' ahead.

title: Iranians express bitterness at war: 'The jailer hasn't changed, my friends, and we are all in line for death'

US consumer inflation surges 3.3% year-on-year amid impact of Iran war
subhead: Gasoline prices surged by 21.2% between February and March – the largest monthly increase since the the government began publishing a gasoline price index in 1967.

title: Ukraine gains upper hand in aerial war against Russia
subhead: Powered by a new generation of drones, Ukrainian forces carried out a record number of strikes in March. Russian oil and gas infrastructure became a primary target, impacting global energy prices.

title: In Budapest, a seven-hour concert aims to hasten Viktor Orban's 'end of the regime'
subhead: Robert Puzser, an intellectual and outspoken critic of the Fidesz party led by Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orban, is organizing this historic free show on Friday, April 10, to urge citizens to turn out for Sunday's legislative elections.

title: AI is reshaping translators' work: 'Translation isn't simply converting words from one language to another'
subhead: New skills are needed, pricing pressure is mounting and 'post-editing' is on the rise. The rise of artificial intelligence is transforming the translation industry and training programs must adapt.

title: Trump's escalatory rhetoric raises questions over his mental health
subhead: Members of Congress, stunned by the crude and genocidal language used by the president, have begun to discuss the US Constitution's 25th Amendment, which allows for the president to be declared unfit and for executive powers to be transferred to the vice president.

title: Macron to meet Pope Leo XIV on Friday to discuss a world in crisis
subhead: The French president and the American pontiff are expected to address conflicts in Lebanon and Iran and contemporary issues such as the dangers posed by the development of artificial intelligence.
